pr.title: run permuter on all remaining low-mismatch functions
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: PR #2586 applied permuter/Codex-assisted cleanup to many remaining low-mismatch functions across fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, library, menu, map, player, and video code. The author states that data-layout-only matches such as replacing inline strings with explicit variables were reverted and should wait until the whole TU is ready to link. The loaded diff shows a broad set of small codegen-oriented edits: local renames, expression reshaping, inline helper extraction, type/sign changes, include pruning, struct typedef recovery, PAD_STACK/register-allocation steering, a tightly-scoped global_optimizer-off pragma, and a few data/symbol declaration adjustments.

renamed locals and preserved copied destination pointer shape with PAD_STACK
introduced recovered struct typedefs and pointer-to-table local for game-mode functions

what_changed: Changed 34 files with 330 additions and 286 deletions according to PR metadata.
Applied a workflow where functions were sorted by remaining mismatch count and Codex ran the author's permuter on a subset of them.
Author explicitly reverted many data-layout-related matches, including inline-string-to-explicit-variable substitutions, because they should wait until the whole TU is ready to link.
Diff examples include local variable renames and expression reshapes, such as camera selected_slot, chained float zero initialization, and decimal 0.003906f multipliers.
Diff examples include helper extraction and pointer aliasing for codegen shape, such as ftCo_800AFC40_inline0, ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate, and a local entry pointer for lbl_803D9498.
Diff examples include type recovery or signature-shape edits, such as gm_1832_8047368C_t, gm_80186F6C_Entry, signed val in gm_IncrementPowerCount, and HSD_ASSERT(0x28A, 0).

Diff examples include matching tactics with explicit comments or visible markers, including PAD_STACK, a global_optimizer-off pragma scoped by push/pop, and a dummy cd != NULL block marked as needed for register allocation.

smart_moves.text: The author treated data-layout-only matches as out of scope for this low-mismatch code pass and reverted examples such as inline string to explicit variable swaps.
The PR kept pragma use tightly scoped around a single function using #pragma push/#pragma global_optimizer off/#pragma pop rather than applying a broad file-level setting.
Several permuter-style temporary names were improved to role names in the loaded diff, such as selected_slot, created_text, color_word, text_color_word, dst, and copied_dst.
ftKb_Init.c removed many unused includes while retaining needed includes, reducing header noise in a large fighter TU.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For broad low-mismatch permuter sweeps, separate text-section/codegen wins from data-layout wins; PR evidence shows the author reverted data-layout-only substitutions and deferred them until a whole TU is ready to link.

Permuter output may produce match-improving but review-sensitive tactics such as dummy null checks, local alias variables, PAD_STACK, and pragma scopes; keep such tactics narrow and explicitly documented when present.
When a permuter improves a function, still clean obvious generated local names into role names where the local role is supported by the surrounding code.

naming_conventions.convention: Use short role names for clear local roles rather than temp_rXX/new_var names; examples in this PR include selected_slot, dst, copied_dst, created_text, color_word, text_color_word, and text_color.
Recovered anonymous table structs may be given function- or symbol-scoped typedef names such as gm_80186F6C_Entry when the table entry layout is used directly in a function.
Address-style globals were preserved for unclear global data such as lbl_8047368C, lbl_803D9498, and lbl_804DA78C rather than renamed semantically beyond field names supported by local use.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Sort remaining functions by mismatch count, then run a permuter on low-mismatch candidates.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: PR body does not provide verifier output for individual matches.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use expression reshaping for codegen, such as chained assignment ordering and multiplication associativity changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Some forms may reduce readability if not justified by objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Extract tiny static inline helpers or local pointer aliases to influence access shape and register allocation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Helper extraction can become artificial if not supported by local authored style or verification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a tightly scoped #pragma global_optimizer off around a single fighter physics function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loaded evidence includes a comment that it is needed for register allocation but no adjacent-function or objdiff ledger.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK in gm_16AE function shape preservation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: PAD_STACK is a matching tactic that needs narrow verifier evidence; none is loaded in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoid landing data-layout-only substitutions during a code matching pass, especially inline-string-to-variable replacements.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The specific reverted hunks are not present in the final diff excerpt, so this is grounded in author body text rather than changed hunks.

Search for PRs touching grkongo.c after PR #2586, since it was the largest changed file but loaded diff excerpt is truncated.

notes: Changed-file metadata is complete for file paths and line counts.
The loaded raw diff is truncated, so several changed files are known only from changed_files metadata, not exact hunks.
There are zero issue comments, review comments, and reviews in the supplied metadata; review_feedback is therefore empty.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but no build, objdiff, checkdiff, or regression output is loaded; individual match claims are not promoted as verified.
The author's body gives strong evidence for the workflow and for deferring data-layout-only matches, but not for exact reviewer intent or final validation status.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Defer data-layout-only wins during broad code matching sweeps
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2586 body reports that permuter/Codex found many data-layout-related matches, such as replacing inline strings with explicitly-defined variables, and that the author reverted those because they should wait until the whole TU is ready to link.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Low-mismatch sorting plus permuter sweep
accepted_candidate_records.text: The author used a script to sort remaining functions by mismatch count and had Codex run the author's permuter on multiple low-mismatch functions, describing the approach as fruitful.

source_update_candidates.title: Historical support for text-before-data in permuter sweeps
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2586 is a concrete historical example supporting the existing text-before-data guidance: during a broad low-mismatch matching pass, the author reverted data-layout-only matches such as inline-string-to-explicit-variable swaps and deferred them until whole-TU link readiness.
source_update_candidates.reason: This does not need to become a new standard because loaded standards already cover it, but it is useful historical support or an example for the curator.

source_update_candidates.title: Require verifier ledger for pragma/PAD_STACK/register-allocation tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2586 contains examples of matching tactics such as #pragma global_optimizer off, PAD_STACK, and a dummy null-check comment for register allocation, but the loaded slice has no objdiff or adjacent regression ledger.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already warn about these tactics; curator may link this PR as an example of why evidence should be recorded.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every changed function in this PR matched; no per-symbol verifier output is loaded.
Do not promote #pragma global_optimizer off, PAD_STACK, dummy null checks, decimal float literals, or local alias variables as preferred style from this PR alone.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; there are no review comments or reviews in the provided slice.
Do not treat the author body as proof of which specific data-layout hunks were reverted; the final diff only shows the landed changes.
